creator: marko.cupac@mimar.rs
text:
(In reply to Ashish SHUKLA from comment #18)

Hi,

I think I made some progress.

TL;DR: The problem appears to be in ejabberdctl.cfg's INET_DIST_INTERFACE. If it is defined to either public IP or loopback IP, ejabberd won't start with jail, but starts on manual restart.

Longer version:

My new server with mostly default config starts fine with jail. I like to have all the services bound to specific IP addresses so I changed config so that all the listeners in ejabberd.yml are bound to IP address of the server. Still, I had something listening on wildcard:

tcp4       0      0 *.48012                *.*                    LISTEN

I noticed that my problematic servers have ejabberdctl.cfg, while new one didn't (only .sample), so I set one up (I redacted IP address and domain below, ERL_EPMD_ADDRESS is server's public IP address, and it resolves, forward and reverse, to host specified in ERLANG_NODE, which I also redacted).

FIREWALL_WINDOW=4200-4210
ERL_EPMD_ADDRESS=192.0.2.226
ERLANG_NODE=ejabberd@im.example.org
EJABBERD_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/local/etc/ejabberd/ejabberd.yml

At this point netstat still shows something listens on wildcard, but on another port:

tcp4       0      0 *.4200                 *.*                    LISTEN

As port is the first one in FIREWALL_WINDOW, I concluded I should also set INET_DIST_INTERFACE in order to bind it to specific IP. However, setting it to either public IP address (192.0.2.226 - redacted), or jail's loopback IP address (127.0.2.226) results in failure to start ejabberd with jail, whereas it starts fine on later manual restart.

My inetrc is default.

Best regards,
